questions.question: What is the role of RTCP in an RTP session?
options: It provides quality feedback (loss, jitter, RTT) between sender and receiver
It sets up the TCP connection
questions.explanation: RTCP (RTP Control Protocol) periodically sends quality reports so the sender knows about packet loss, jitter, and round-trip time at the receiver.

questions.question: How is round-trip time calculated from RTCP reports?
options: RTT = current_NTP_time - LSR - DLSR, using timestamps exchanged in SR and RR
It cannot be calculated from RTCP
questions.explanation: The sender includes its NTP timestamp in SR. The receiver echoes it as LSR and adds DLSR (delay since receiving that SR). The sender computes RTT = now - LSR - DLSR.

questions.question: What does the RTP sequence number detect?
options: Packet loss and reordering by tracking gaps in the sequence
questions.explanation: The sequence number increments by 1 per packet. Gaps indicate lost packets, and out-of-order values indicate reordering.

questions.question: What does the interarrival jitter field in an RTCP RR represent?
options: Smoothed variation in packet interarrival time, measured in RTP timestamp units
questions.explanation: Jitter is the smoothed estimate of the variation in interarrival time between consecutive packets, expressed in RTP timestamp units (not absolute delay).
